lesson: A LOW MIX alarm with a full hopper, rising barrel temp and open-circuit pump windings is a burned-out mix pump. It runs silently when healthy, so check the windings.
symptoms: MIX LOW alarm with a visibly full hopper.
Product watery, then nothing at all.

tests.error-log: MIX LOW alarm active. No pump fault code — the pump just isn't running.
tests.temp-probe: Barrel temp trending upward. Hopper at correct fill level.
tests.continuity-test: Mix pump motor windings read open-circuit. Motor has burned out.

symptoms: MIX LOW alarm, hopper full, and no pump hum when the machine calls for mix.

tests.error-log: MIX LOW active. No pump fault code — the controller doesn't know the pump is dead.
tests.continuity-test: Pump motor windings read open-circuit. It's burned out.

symptoms: Staff noticed a hot-electrics smell near the base yesterday.
Now MIX LOW with a full hopper and watery product.

tests.inspect-beater: Pump housing discoloured and smelling of varnish. That motor cooked itself.
tests.continuity-test: Open circuit across the pump windings. Gone.

flavour: The pump was doing nothing because the pump was nothing.
authenticityNote: Mix pump motor burnout presents identically to a blocked line or a dead level sensor. Because the pump runs silently when healthy, its absence isn't obvious until you check the windings.
